ctx.name: Priya
ctx.notes: Just shipped the payments refactor on Monday — big win, team noticed. Looks a bit flat this week, quieter than usual in standup. Mentioned in passing they'd like to do more mentoring; we haven't talked about that in a few months. Next quarter they'll probably be pulled into the billing rewrite, which they haven't heard about yet.

focusPoints.label: Celebrating the payments refactor win
focusPoints.reason: Just shipped the payments refactor — big win, team noticed. It's important to acknowledge contributions to maintain motivation.

focusPoints.label: Exploring energy levels this week
focusPoints.reason: Looks a bit flat this week, quieter than usual in standup. Could be fatigue, personal matters, or just a quieter stretch — worth checking in.

focusPoints.label: Interest in mentoring opportunities
focusPoints.reason: Mentioned in passing they'd like to do more mentoring. It's been a few months since discussing this — worth exploring how to support that.

focusPoints.label: Upcoming billing rewrite involvement
focusPoints.reason: Next quarter they'll probably be pulled into the billing rewrite, which they haven't heard about yet. Clarifying expectations and involvement could be beneficial.

focusPoints.label: Any blockers on current tasks?
focusPoints.reason: No specific signal — standard check-in topic. It's good practice to see if there are any obstacles they're facing.

transcript.question: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
transcript.answer: Last two weeks were intense — shipping payments was a sprint but it landed clean. Mostly good energy but definitely coasting a bit this week.

transcript.question: What do you think has contributed to your quieter demeanor this week?
transcript.answer: Cleanup and docs for payments, reviewing PRs for the new team members. Not much stretching me right now.

transcript.question: How did it feel to ship the payments refactor this week?
transcript.answer: Nothing big. A lot of context-switching on reviews — probably fine, but it adds up.

transcript.question: How do you feel the team has responded to the payments refactor?
transcript.answer: I'd like to do more mentoring. I brought it up maybe three months ago and nothing came of it. I'm not pushing.

transcript.question: Do you feel you have the bandwidth right now to take on mentoring responsibilities?
transcript.answer: Honestly — the billing rewrite. I've heard whispers but nobody's talked to me about it directly. That's a bit weird.

transcript.question: What are your initial thoughts about getting involved in the billing rewrite?
transcript.answer: Payments was real — I owned the migration plan and made the call on the dual-write window. I'm proud of how that went.

transcript.question: What would help you feel more prepared for the upcoming billing rewrite?
transcript.answer: Growth-wise I think I'm flat. I'm the most senior IC on the team and there's no one really pushing me.

transcript.question: Are there any blockers on your current tasks that we should address?
transcript.answer: More clarity on scope would help. And hearing about big projects before they're locked in, not after.

I'd like to do more mentoring. I brought it up maybe three months ago and nothing came of it. I'm not pushing.

Payments was real — I owned the migration plan and made the call on the dual-write window. I'm proud of how that went.
Growth-wise I think I'm flat. I'm the most senior IC on the team and there's no one really pushing me.
Honestly — the billing rewrite. I've heard whispers but nobody's talked to me about it directly. That's a bit weird.

Mentioned in passing they'd like to do more mentoring; we haven't talked about that in a few months. Next quarter they'll probably be pulled into the billing rewrite, which they haven't heard about yet.
